title: California Employment Development Department Penalty for Late State Unemployment Insurance Payment
detailed_summary: Infosys disclosed that it received communication from the Employment Development Department, State of California, regarding a penalty of USD 11,041.03 for late payment of State Unemployment Insurance for Q4FY25. The company stated that there is no material impact on its financials, operations, or other activities.

key_facts.fact: The Employment Development Department, State of California levied a total penalty of USD 11,041.03.
The penalty was levied due to late payment of State Unemployment Insurance for Q4FY25.
Infosys stated that there is no material impact on financials, operations or other activities of the Company.

litigation_or_regulatory_matters.authority_or_counterparty: Employment Development Department, State of California
litigation_or_regulatory_matters.description: Penalty levied due to late payment of State Unemployment Insurance for Q4FY25.

important_text_spans.reason: Supports the amount of the regulatory penalty.

important_text_spans.text: None, there is no material impact on financials, operations or other activities of the Company.
important_text_spans.reason: Supports the stated lack of material business or financial impact.

search_keywords: Employment Development Department State of California
sample_questions: What penalty did Infosys receive from the Employment Development Department, State of California?
Why was Infosys penalized for Q4FY25 State Unemployment Insurance?
Did Infosys say the California penalty had any material financial or operational impact?
